UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile and basic information within the UniLink Access application. This entity stores details essential for user identification, communication, and displaying personalized content on the dashboard.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, matching the Firebase Auth UID.

email.description: The user's unique email address, used for login and password recovery.

role.description: The user's role within the application.

carreraId.description: Optional. The ID of the 'carrera' (academic program) the user is associated with. Relevant for 'Jefe de carrera' and 'Docente' roles.

status.description: The status of the user account.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user profile was updated.

Session.title: Session
Session.description: Manages user login sessions, enabling persistent access and tracking active user interactions with the UniLink Access application. This entity helps in maintaining secure and continuous user experience.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Session entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile associated with this session. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Session)

authToken.description: A unique token used to authenticate the session.

expiresAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the session is set to expire, requiring re-authentication.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the session was initiated.

isActive.description: A boolean flag indicating whether the session is currently active.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Each document ID (`userProfileId`) matches the authenticated user's UID. Access is restricted to the authenticated user matching `userProfileId` in the path. This structure supports Authorization Independence for user-owned data via path-based ownership and enables secure `get` operations for a specific user's profile.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the user's profile, which directly corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID (`request.auth.uid`).

definition.description: Stores active login sessions for a specific user. Each session document's access is bound to its parent `userProfileId`. This structure supports Authorization Independence by providing the `userProfileId` directly in the path, allowing rules to compare `request.auth.uid` with `userProfileId`. It enables secure `list` operations for a user to retrieve all their own sessions.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent user's profile, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication UID (`request.auth.uid`).

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific user session.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling to ensure a secure, scalable, and easily debuggable system for UniLink Access. The design prioritizes robust security rules and efficient data access.

**Authorization Independence:**
*   **User Profiles (`/userProfiles/{userProfileId}`):** Each user's profile is stored in a top-level collection, with the document ID (`userProfileId`) directly corresponding to the authenticated user's UID (`request.auth.uid`). This direct mapping means that authorization rules for reading or writing a user's profile can directly compare `request.auth.uid` to the `userProfileId` wildcard in the path, eliminating any need for `get()` operations to fetch parent documents or other user data for authorization. This ensures atomic operations and simplifies rule debugging.
*   **User Sessions (`/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/sessions/{sessionId}`):** Session documents are stored as a subcollection under the respective user's profile. The `userProfileId` wildcard in the path inherently provides the authorization context for these documents. Although the `Session` entity itself contains a `userId` field, the primary authorization check will leverage the `userProfileId` from the path (`request.auth.uid == userProfileId`). This denormalization of the `userId` into the path (and implicitly within the document via its structure) makes session access rules simple and avoids `get()` calls, adhering to the principle of Authorization Independence.

**Querying for Authorization Purposes (QAPs):**
*   **User Profiles:** Users can `get` their own profile securely by matching `request.auth.uid` to the `userProfileId` in the path. Listing all profiles (`/userProfiles`) would typically be restricted or require specific administrative roles, as this is private data and rules are not filters. However, a user can securely `get` their specific document.
*   **User Sessions:** Users can securely `list` their own active sessions by querying the `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/sessions` subcollection. The security rules will ensure that `request.auth.uid` matches the `userProfileId` in the path, preventing users from accessing or listing sessions belonging to other users. This approach enables secure, efficient queries without relying on client-side filtering or inefficient server-side rule filtering.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:**
*   **Private Data:** Both `UserProfile` and `Session` entities represent private, user-owned data. The chosen hierarchical path structure (`/userProfiles/{userProfileId}` and `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/sessions/{sessionId}`) directly implements the "Private Data" and "Hierarchical Paths for User-Owned Data" mandates. All documents within `/userProfiles` share the same owner-only security posture, as do all documents within `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/sessions` for a given user. This minimizes complexity in security rules and ensures that data with different access requirements are not co-mingled.
